overview.tagline: A quiet, upscale borough with top-tier schools and a serene suburban setting
overview.description: Norwood is a small, affluent residential borough in the northernmost part of Bergen County, bordering New York State to the north. With a population just over 5,700, the borough offers a tranquil suburban lifestyle with spacious lots, tree-lined streets, and a strong sense of privacy. Norwood is part of the highly regarded Northern Valley Regional High School District and is known for its excellent schools, low density, and proximity to the Palisades Interstate Park. The community attracts families seeking top schools and a quiet, semi-rural atmosphere within commuting distance of New York City.
overview.schoolDistrict: Norwood Public Schools / Northern Valley Regional
highlights: Part of the top-ranked Northern Valley Regional High School District
Spacious lots and a semi-rural residential character
Close proximity to Palisades Interstate Park hiking and nature
Very low crime rate and safe, family-friendly environment
NJ Transit Pascack Valley Line train station for NYC commuters

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Bergen County conducts periodic revaluations. Norwood's larger lot sizes and proximity to the Palisades contribute to stable property values.
realEstate.recentTrends: Norwood has seen consistent appreciation, driven by demand from families for Northern Valley district schools. The market features larger homes on generous lots compared to denser Bergen County communities. Inventory remains limited, making the market competitive.
neighborhoodNotes: Piermont Road corridor — Borough's main road with a mix of homes and small businesses
Livingston Avenue/Summit Street — Established family neighborhoods near the school
Blanch Avenue area — Spacious lots with a wooded, private feel
Tappan Road section — Properties near the New York border with larger lots
Near the train station — Convenient for commuters on the Pascack Valley Line

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection provided by a contracted hauler for the Borough.
details: Trash pickup occurs twice per week
Containers at curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Maximum 50 lbs per container with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by scheduling with DPW
Holiday schedule posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Recycling collected on a regular schedule by the Borough's contractor.
details: Commingled recyclables collected biweekly
Paper and cardboard on alternate weeks
Yard waste collected seasonally from spring through fall
Recycling bins provided by the Borough
Special recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water provided by Veolia Water. Sewer service through the Borough and Northwest Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Veolia Water handles water supply and billing
Sewer service through NBCUA
Some properties on private well and/or septic systems
Annual water quality reports available
Emergency water issues handled 24/7 by Veolia

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: Building Department handles permits, inspections, and code enforcement.
details: Permits required for construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning Board reviews variance applications
Applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Borough manages commuter parking at the train station and street regulations.
details: Commuter parking permits available at the Norwood train station
Waitlist may apply for train station parking
Street parking subject to posted restrictions
Overnight parking restrictions enforced
Snow emergency parking rules in effect

additionalServices.title: Norwood Public Library
additionalServices.description: Community library serving residents with books, programs, and digital resources.
details: Located at 453 Tappan Road
Children's programs, story times, and summer reading
Free Wi-Fi and computer access
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Norwood enforces noise regulations to preserve the quiet, residential nature of the borough.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM weekdays
Saturday construction 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or holidays
Power equipment restricted to daytime hours
Loud music and gatherings subject to limits after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Properties must be maintained to borough standards to preserve the community's character.
keyRules: Lawns and landscaping must be kept maintained
Properties must be free of debris and junk
Structures must be kept in good condition
Sidewalks (where present) must be cleared of snow within 24 hours
Septic systems must be properly maintained where applicable

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Pet owners must follow licensing and leash requirements.
keyRules: Dogs must be licensed annually
Dogs must be leashed off owner's property
Pet waste must be cleaned up immediately
Excessive barking subject to complaints
Wildlife feeding restrictions may apply

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking rules are enforced throughout the borough.
keyRules: Overnight parking on streets prohibited from 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM
Vehicles must be moved during snow emergencies
No parking near fire hydrants or intersections
Commuter parking permits required at the train station
Violations subject to fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with borough registration and maintenance standards.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered
Certificate of habitability required
Properties must meet code standards
Landlords responsible for maintenance
Short-term rentals subject to zoning restrictions

other.title: Septic System Regulations
other.summary: Properties with private septic systems must comply with maintenance requirements.
keyRules: Septic systems must be pumped and inspected on a regular schedule
Failing systems must be repaired or replaced promptly
Connection to public sewer is required when available within a certain distance
Bergen County Board of Health oversees septic compliance
other.source: Borough of Norwood Code / Bergen County Health Department

schools.publicDistrict: Norwood Public Schools / Northern Valley Regional High School District

publicSchools.name: Norwood Public School
publicSchools.notes: Borough's sole public school for K-8 students; known for strong academics and a supportive community

publicSchools.name: Northern Valley Regional High School at Demarest
publicSchools.notes: Regional high school in Demarest; consistently ranked among NJ's top public high schools

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 15 miles

whyMoveHere: Top-ranked Northern Valley Regional High School at Demarest
Quiet, semi-rural atmosphere with spacious lots
NJ Transit Pascack Valley Line train station in the borough
Proximity to Palisades Interstate Park for hiking and outdoor recreation
Very safe community with low crime rates

commuteToNYC.duration: 55-75 minutes to Hoboken, then PATH to NYC
commuteToNYC.notes: The Norwood station on the Pascack Valley Line provides rail service to Hoboken Terminal, where riders connect via PATH to Midtown or Downtown Manhattan. Bus service is also available. Drivers can access the George Washington Bridge via the Palisades Interstate Parkway or Route 9W.

costOfLiving.summary: Norwood is an upper-middle-income community with home prices reflecting the quality of schools and the spacious residential environment.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Above the state average for housing and property taxes

pros: Excellent schools in the Northern Valley district
Train station in the borough for commuters
Spacious lots and semi-rural feel uncommon in Bergen County
Access to Palisades Interstate Park for nature and recreation
Very safe and quiet residential environment
cons: Very limited commercial and dining options
Pascack Valley Line has limited off-peak service
Higher home prices and property taxes
Some properties on septic systems rather than public sewer
Remote from Bergen County's larger towns and cultural centers

restaurants.name: The Office Tavern Grill
restaurants.description: Local sports bar and restaurant with American fare, burgers, and craft beers.

restaurants.name: Norwood Pizza
restaurants.description: Neighborhood pizzeria serving traditional pies, heroes, and Italian specialties.

restaurants.name: Sakura Japanese Restaurant
restaurants.description: Japanese restaurant with sushi, teriyaki, and traditional Japanese dishes.

parks.name: Norwood Community Park
parks.description: Borough park with sports fields, playground, and picnic areas.

parks.name: Veterans Memorial Park
parks.description: Small memorial park honoring veterans with a peaceful setting.

parks.name: Palisades Interstate Park (nearby)
parks.description: Scenic park along the Hudson River Palisades with world-class hiking trails, lookout points, and picnic areas.

events.name: Norwood Day
events.description: Annual community celebration with food, music, games, and family activities.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Festive community gathering with tree lighting and seasonal refreshments.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Annual parade honoring veterans with community participation.

attractions.name: Palisades Interstate Park
attractions.description: Stunning cliffside park along the Hudson River offering hiking, scenic overlooks, and access to the shore trail.

attractions.name: Tappan (NY) Historic Sites
attractions.description: Just across the New York border, visit Revolutionary War-era sites in historic Tappan including the DeWint House, Washington's headquarters.

shoppingAreas: Nearby Closter and Old Tappan shopping

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450
